tEn: Universal Language Model Fine-tuning for Text Classification
tZh: 通用语言模型微调用于文本分类
sEn: ULMFiT enables effective transfer learning for any NLP task, achieving state-of-the-art results with significantly less data.
sZh: ULMFiT实现了对任何NLP任务的有效迁移学习，用更少的数据取得了最先进的结果。
absEn: Inductive transfer learning has greatly impacted computer vision, but existing approaches in NLP still require task-specific modifications and training from scratch. We propose Universal Language Model Fine-tuning (ULMFiT), an effective transfer learning method that can be applied to any task in NLP, and introduce techniques that are key for fine-tuning a language model. Our method significantly outperforms the state-of-the-art on six text classification tasks, reducing the error by 18-24% on the majority of datasets. Furthermore, with only 100 labeled examples, it matches the performance of training from scratch on 100x more data. We open-source our pretrained models and code.
absZh: 归纳迁移学习极大地影响了计算机视觉，但NLP中的现有方法仍然需要针对特定任务的修改和从头开始训练。我们提出了通用语言模型微调（ULMFiT），这是一种有效的迁移学习方法，可以应用于NLP中的任何任务，并介绍了对语言模型进行微调的关键技术。我们的方法在六个文本分类任务上显著优于现有技术，在大多数数据集上将错误率降低了18-24%。此外，仅使用100个标记示例，其性能就与在100倍数据上从头训练相匹配。我们开源了预训练模型和代码。

contrib.en: Proposes Universal Language Model Fine-tuning (ULMFiT), a transfer learning method applicable to any NLP task.
contrib.zh: 提出通用语言模型微调（ULMFiT），一种可应用于任何NLP任务的迁移学习方法。

contrib.en: Introduces discriminative fine-tuning, slanted triangular learning rates, and gradual unfreezing to prevent catastrophic forgetting.
contrib.zh: 引入判别式微调、倾斜三角学习率和逐步解冻，以防止灾难性遗忘。

contrib.en: Achieves state-of-the-art on six text classification tasks, reducing error by 18-24% on most datasets.
contrib.zh: 在六个文本分类任务上达到最先进水平，在大多数数据集上错误率降低18-24%。

contrib.en: Matches performance of training from scratch on 100x more data with only 100 labeled examples.
contrib.zh: 仅用100个标注样本即可匹配在100倍数据上从头训练的性能。

contrib.en: Open-sources pretrained models and code to enable wider adoption.
contrib.zh: 开源预训练模型和代码以促进更广泛采用。

limits.en: Method evaluated only on text classification tasks, not on sequence labeling or other NLP tasks.
limits.zh: 方法仅在文本分类任务上评估，未在序列标注或其他NLP任务上测试。

limits.en: Requires a large general-domain corpus (Wikitext-103) for pretraining, which may not be available for all languages.
limits.zh: 需要大型通用领域语料库（Wikitext-103）进行预训练，并非所有语言都可用。

limits.en: Fine-tuning techniques (discriminative learning rates, STLR) require careful hyperparameter tuning.
limits.zh: 微调技术（判别式学习率、STLR）需要仔细的超参数调优。

limits.en: Performance on very small datasets (e.g., TREC-6) may not be statistically significant due to limited test size.
limits.zh: 在非常小的数据集（如TREC-6）上的性能可能因测试集规模有限而不具有统计显著性。

limits.en: Method relies on LSTM architecture; performance may vary with other architectures like Transformer.
limits.zh: 方法依赖LSTM架构；使用Transformer等其他架构时性能可能不同。

items.en: Inductive transfer learning has greatly impacted computer vision, but existing approaches in NLP still require task-specific modifications and training from scratch. We propose Universal Language Model Fine-tuning (ULMFiT), an effective transfer learning method that can be applied to any task in NLP, and introduce techniques that are key for fine-tuning a language model. Our method significantly outperforms the state-of-the-art on six text classification tasks, reducing the error by 18-24% on the majority of datasets. Furthermore, with only 100100100 labeled examples, it matches the performance of training from scratch on 100×100\times more data. We open-source our pretrained models and code222http://nlp.fast.ai/ulmfit..
items.zh: 归纳迁移学习对计算机视觉产生了巨大影响，但自然语言处理中的现有方法仍需要针对特定任务的修改和从头训练。我们提出了通用语言模型微调（ULMFiT），这是一种有效的迁移学习方法，可应用于自然语言处理中的任何任务，并介绍了对语言模型进行微调的关键技术。我们的方法在六个文本分类任务上显著优于现有技术，在大多数数据集上错误率降低了18-24%。此外，仅使用100个标注样本，其性能就与在100倍数据上从头训练相匹配。我们开源了预训练模型和代码。

items.en: Inductive transfer learning has had a large impact on computer vision (CV). Applied CV models (including object detection, classification, and segmentation) are rarely trained from scratch, but instead are fine-tuned from models that have been pretrained on ImageNet, MS-COCO, and other datasets Sharif Razavian et al. (2014); Long et al. (2015a); He et al. (2016); Huang et al. (2017).
items.zh: 归纳迁移学习对计算机视觉产生了巨大影响。应用型计算机视觉模型（包括目标检测、分类和分割）很少从头训练，而是从在ImageNet、MS-COCO等数据集上预训练的模型进行微调。

items.en: Text classification is a category of Natural Language Processing (NLP) tasks with real-world applications such as spam, fraud, and bot detection Jindal and Liu (2007); Ngai et al. (2011); Chu et al. (2012), emergency response Caragea et al. (2011), and commercial document classification, such as for legal discovery Roitblat et al. (2010).
items.zh: 文本分类是自然语言处理任务的一个类别，具有实际应用，如垃圾邮件、欺诈和机器人检测，应急响应，以及商业文档分类（如法律发现）。

items.en: While Deep Learning models have achieved state-of-the-art on many NLP tasks, these models are trained from scratch, requiring large datasets, and days to converge. Research in NLP focused mostly on transductive transfer Blitzer et al. (2007). For inductive transfer, fine-tuning pretrained word embeddings Mikolov et al. (2013), a simple transfer technique that only targets a model’s first layer, has had a large impact in practice and is used in most state-of-the-art models. Recent approaches that concatenate embeddings derived from other tasks with the input at different layers Peters et al. (2017); McCann et al. (2017); Peters et al. (2018) still train the main task model from scratch and treat pretrained embeddings as fixed parameters, limiting their usefulness.
items.zh: 虽然深度学习模型在许多自然语言处理任务上取得了最先进的结果，但这些模型是从头训练的，需要大型数据集和数天才能收敛。自然语言处理的研究主要集中在传导迁移上。对于归纳迁移，微调预训练词嵌入是一种简单的迁移技术，仅针对模型的第一层，在实践中产生了巨大影响，并被大多数最先进模型使用。最近的方法将其他任务派生的嵌入与输入在不同层连接起来，但仍然从头训练主任务模型，并将预训练嵌入视为固定参数，限制了其有用性。

items.en: In light of the benefits of pretraining Erhan et al. (2010), we should be able to do better than randomly initializing the remaining parameters of our models. However, inductive transfer via fine-tuning has been unsuccessful for NLP Mou et al. (2016). Dai and Le (2015) first proposed fine-tuning a language model (LM) but require millions of in-domain documents to achieve good performance, which severely limits its applicability.
items.zh: 鉴于预训练的好处，我们应该能够比随机初始化模型剩余参数做得更好。然而，通过微调进行归纳迁移在自然语言处理中并不成功。Dai和Le首先提出了微调语言模型，但需要数百万领域内文档才能获得良好性能，这严重限制了其适用性。

items.en: We show that not the idea of LM fine-tuning but our lack of knowledge of how to train them effectively has been hindering wider adoption. LMs overfit to small datasets and suffered catastrophic forgetting when fine-tuned with a classifier. Compared to CV, NLP models are typically more shallow and thus require different fine-tuning methods.
items.zh: 我们表明，阻碍更广泛采用的不是语言模型微调的想法，而是我们缺乏如何有效训练它们的知识。语言模型在小数据集上过拟合，并且在用分类器微调时遭受灾难性遗忘。与计算机视觉相比，自然语言处理模型通常更浅，因此需要不同的微调方法。

items.en: We propose a new method, Universal Language Model Fine-tuning (ULMFiT) that addresses these issues and enables robust inductive transfer learning for any NLP task, akin to fine-tuning ImageNet models: The same 3-layer LSTM architecture—with the same hyperparameters and no additions other than tuned dropout hyperparameters—outperforms highly engineered models and transfer learning approaches on six widely studied text classification tasks. On IMDb, with 100100100 labeled examples, ULMFiT matches the performance of training from scratch with 10×10\times and—given 505050k unlabeled examples—with 100×100\times more data.
items.zh: 我们提出了一种新方法，通用语言模型微调（ULMFiT），它解决了这些问题，并实现了对任何自然语言处理任务的鲁棒归纳迁移学习，类似于微调ImageNet模型：相同的3层LSTM架构——具有相同的超参数，除了调整后的dropout超参数外没有其他添加——在六个广泛研究的文本分类任务上优于高度工程化的模型和迁移学习方法。在IMDb上，使用100个标注样本，ULMFiT的性能与在10倍数据上从头训练相当，并且给定50k个未标注样本，与在100倍数据上从头训练相当。

items.en: Our contributions are the following: 1) We propose Universal Language Model Fine-tuning (ULMFiT), a method that can be used to achieve CV-like transfer learning for any task for NLP. 2) We propose discriminative fine-tuning, slanted triangular learning rates, and gradual unfreezing, novel techniques to retain previous knowledge and avoid catastrophic forgetting during fine-tuning. 3) We significantly outperform the state-of-the-art on six representative text classification datasets, with an error reduction of 18-24% on the majority of datasets. 4) We show that our method enables extremely sample-efficient transfer learning and perform an extensive ablation analysis. 5) We make the pretrained models and our code available to enable wider adoption.
items.zh: 我们的贡献如下：1）我们提出了通用语言模型微调（ULMFiT），一种可用于实现类似计算机视觉的迁移学习的方法，适用于任何自然语言处理任务。2）我们提出了判别式微调、倾斜三角学习率和逐步解冻，这些新技术用于在微调期间保留先前知识并避免灾难性遗忘。3）我们在六个代表性文本分类数据集上显著优于现有技术，在大多数数据集上错误率降低了18-24%。4）我们表明我们的方法实现了极其样本高效的迁移学习，并进行了广泛的消融分析。5）我们公开了预训练模型和代码，以促进更广泛的采用。

items.en: Features in deep neural networks in CV have been observed to transition from general to task-specific from the first to the last layer Yosinski et al. (2014). For this reason, most work in CV focuses on transferring the first layers of the model Long et al. (2015b). Sharif Razavian et al. (2014) achieve state-of-the-art results using features of an ImageNet model as input to a simple classifier. In recent years, this approach has been superseded by fine-tuning either the last Donahue et al. (2014) or several of the last layers of a pretrained model and leaving the remaining layers frozen Long et al. (2015a).
items.zh: 在计算机视觉中，深度神经网络的特征从第一层到最后一层被观察到从通用过渡到任务特定。因此，计算机视觉中的大多数工作集中在迁移模型的前几层。Sharif Razavian等人使用ImageNet模型的特征作为简单分类器的输入，取得了最先进的结果。近年来，这种方法已被微调最后几层或预训练模型的最后几层而冻结其余层所取代。

items.en: In NLP, only recently have methods been proposed that go beyond transferring word embeddings. The prevailing approach is to pretrain embeddings that capture additional context via other tasks. Embeddings at different levels are then used as features, concatenated either with the word embeddings or with the inputs at intermediate layers. This method is known as hypercolumns Hariharan et al. (2015) in CV333A hypercolumn at a pixel in CV is the vector of activations of all CNN units above that pixel. In analogy, a hypercolumn for a word or sentence in NLP is the concatenation of embeddings at different layers in a pretrained model. and is used by Peters et al. (2017), Peters et al. (2018), Wieting and Gimpel (2017), Conneau et al. (2017), and McCann et al. (2017) who use language modeling, paraphrasing, entailment, and Machine Translation (MT) respectively for pretraining. Specifically, Peters et al. (2018) require engineered custom architectures, while we show state-of-the-art performance with the same basic architecture across a range of tasks. In CV, hypercolumns have been nearly entirely superseded by end-to-end fine-tuning Long et al. (2015a).
items.zh: 在自然语言处理中，直到最近才提出了超越迁移词嵌入的方法。主流方法是通过其他任务预训练捕获额外上下文的嵌入。然后，不同层次的嵌入被用作特征，与词嵌入或中间层的输入连接。这种方法在计算机视觉中被称为超列，并被Peters等人、Peters等人、Wieting和Gimpel、Conneau等人以及McCann等人使用，他们分别使用语言建模、释义、蕴含和机器翻译进行预训练。具体来说，Peters等人需要工程化的自定义架构，而我们展示了在多种任务上使用相同基本架构的最先进性能。在计算机视觉中，超列几乎已被端到端微调完全取代。

items.en: A related direction is multi-task learning (MTL) Caruana (1993). This is the approach taken by Rei (2017) and Liu et al. (2018) who add a language modeling objective to the model that is trained jointly with the main task model. MTL requires the tasks to be trained from scratch every time, which makes it inefficient and often requires careful weighting of the task-specific objective functions Chen et al. (2017).
items.zh: 一个相关的方向是多任务学习。这是Rei和Liu等人采用的方法，他们向与主任务模型联合训练的模型添加语言建模目标。多任务学习要求每次从头训练任务，这使其效率低下，并且通常需要仔细权衡任务特定目标函数。

items.en: Fine-tuning has been used successfully to transfer between similar tasks, e.g. in QA Min et al. (2017), for distantly supervised sentiment analysis Severyn and Moschitti (2015), or MT domains Sennrich et al. (2015) but has been shown to fail between unrelated ones Mou et al. (2016). Dai and Le (2015) also fine-tune a language model, but overfit with 101010k labeled examples and require millions of in-domain documents for good performance. In contrast, ULMFiT leverages general-domain pretraining and novel fine-tuning techniques to prevent overfitting even with only 100100100 labeled examples and achieves state-of-the-art results also on small datasets.
items.zh: 微调已成功用于相似任务之间的迁移，例如在问答、远程监督情感分析或机器翻译领域，但已被证明在无关任务之间失败。Dai和Le也微调了语言模型，但在10k个标注样本上过拟合，并且需要数百万领域内文档才能获得良好性能。相比之下，ULMFiT利用通用领域预训练和新颖的微调技术来防止过拟合，即使只有100个标注样本，也能在小数据集上取得最先进的结果。

items.en: We are interested in the most general inductive transfer learning setting for NLP Pan and Yang (2010): Given a static source task 𝒯Ssubscript𝒯𝑆\mathcal{T}_{S} and any target task 𝒯Tsubscript𝒯𝑇\mathcal{T}_{T} with 𝒯S≠𝒯Tsubscript𝒯𝑆subscript𝒯𝑇\mathcal{T}_{S}\neq\mathcal{T}_{T}, we would like to improve performance on 𝒯Tsubscript𝒯𝑇\mathcal{T}_{T}. Language modeling can be seen as the ideal source task and a counterpart of ImageNet for NLP: It captures many facets of language relevant for downstream tasks, such as long-term dependencies Linzen et al. (2016), hierarchical relations Gulordava et al. (2018), and sentiment Radford et al. (2017). In contrast to tasks like MT McCann et al. (2017) and entailment Conneau et al. (2017), it provides data in near-unlimited quantities for most domains and languages. Additionally, a pretrained LM can be easily adapted to the idiosyncrasies of a target task, which we show significantly improves performance (see Section 5). Moreover, language modeling already is a key component of existing tasks such as MT and dialogue modeling. Formally, language modeling induces a hypothesis space ℋℋ\mathcal{H} that should be useful for many other NLP tasks Vapnik and Kotz (1982); Baxter (2000).
items.zh: 我们感兴趣的是自然语言处理中最一般的归纳迁移学习设置：给定静态源任务和任何目标任务，我们希望提高目标任务的性能。语言建模可以被视为理想的源任务和自然语言处理中ImageNet的对应物：它捕获了与下游任务相关的语言的许多方面，如长期依赖、层次关系和情感。与机器翻译和蕴含等任务相比，它为大多数领域和语言提供了近乎无限量的数据。此外，预训练的语言模型可以轻松适应目标任务的特性，我们表明这显著提高了性能（见第5节）。此外，语言建模已经是现有任务（如机器翻译和对话建模）的关键组成部分。形式上，语言模型诱导了一个假设空间，该空间应该对许多其他自然语言处理任务有用。

items.en: We propose Universal Language Model Fine-tuning (ULMFiT), which pretrains a language model (LM) on a large general-domain corpus and fine-tunes it on the target task using novel techniques. The method is universal in the sense that it meets these practical criteria: 1) It works across tasks varying in document size, number, and label type; 2) it uses a single architecture and training process; 3) it requires no custom feature engineering or preprocessing; and 4) it does not require additional in-domain documents or labels.
items.zh: 我们提出了通用语言模型微调（ULMFiT），它在大型通用领域语料库上预训练语言模型，并使用新技术在目标任务上对其进行微调。该方法在满足以下实际标准方面是通用的：1）它适用于文档大小、数量和标签类型各异的任务；2）它使用单一架构和训练过程；3）它不需要自定义特征工程或预处理；4）它不需要额外的领域内文档或标签。

items.en: In our experiments, we use the state-of-the-art language model AWD-LSTM Merity et al. (2017a), a regular LSTM (with no attention, short-cut connections, or other sophisticated additions) with various tuned dropout hyperparameters. Analogous to CV, we expect that downstream performance can be improved by using higher-performance language models in the future.
items.zh: 在我们的实验中，我们使用了最先进的语言模型AWD-LSTM，这是一个带有各种调整后的dropout超参数的常规LSTM（没有注意力、捷径连接或其他复杂添加）。类似于计算机视觉，我们预计未来使用更高性能的语言模型可以改善下游性能。

items.en: ULMFiT consists of the following steps, which we show in Figure 1: a) General-domain LM pretraining (§3.1); b) target task LM fine-tuning (§3.2); and c) target task classifier fine-tuning (§3.3). We discuss these in the following sections.
items.zh: ULMFiT包括以下步骤，如图1所示：a）通用领域语言模型预训练（§3.1）；b）目标任务语言模型微调（§3.2）；c）目标任务分类器微调（§3.3）。我们将在以下章节中讨论这些步骤。

full.secZh: 3.1 通用领域语言模型预训练

items.en: An ImageNet-like corpus for language should be large and capture general properties of language. We pretrain the language model on Wikitext-103 Merity et al. (2017b) consisting of 28,595 preprocessed Wikipedia articles and 103 million words. Pretraining is most beneficial for tasks with small datasets and enables generalization even with 100100100 labeled examples. We leave the exploration of more diverse pretraining corpora to future work, but expect that they would boost performance. While this stage is the most expensive, it only needs to be performed once and improves performance and convergence of downstream models.
items.zh: 类似ImageNet的语言语料库应该足够大并捕获语言的通用属性。我们在Wikitext-103上预训练语言模型，该数据集包含28,595篇预处理过的维基百科文章和1.03亿个单词。预训练对小型数据集的任务最有益，即使只有100个标注样本也能实现泛化。我们将更多样化预训练语料库的探索留给未来工作，但预计它们会提升性能。虽然这一阶段最昂贵，但它只需要执行一次，并改善下游模型的性能和收敛速度。

full.secZh: 3.2 目标任务语言模型微调

items.en: No matter how diverse the general-domain data used for pretraining is, the data of the target task will likely come from a different distribution. We thus fine-tune the LM on data of the target task. Given a pretrained general-domain LM, this stage converges faster as it only needs to adapt to the idiosyncrasies of the target data, and it allows us to train a robust LM even for small datasets. We propose discriminative fine-tuning and slanted triangular learning rates for fine-tuning the LM, which we introduce in the following.
items.zh: 无论用于预训练的通用领域数据多么多样化，目标任务的数据很可能来自不同的分布。因此，我们在目标任务的数据上对语言模型进行微调。给定一个预训练的通用领域语言模型，这一阶段收敛更快，因为它只需要适应目标数据的特性，并且即使对于小数据集，我们也能训练出鲁棒的语言模型。我们提出了用于微调语言模型的判别式微调和倾斜三角学习率，下面将介绍这些方法。

items.en: As different layers capture different types of information Yosinski et al. (2014), they should be fine-tuned to different extents. To this end, we propose a novel fine-tuning method, discriminative fine-tuning444 An unrelated method of the same name exists for deep Boltzmann machines Salakhutdinov and Hinton (2009)..
items.zh: 由于不同层捕获不同类型的信息（Yosinski et al., 2014），它们应该被微调到不同程度。为此，我们提出了一种新颖的微调方法，即判别式微调。

items.en: Instead of using the same learning rate for all layers of the model, discriminative fine-tuning allows us to tune each layer with different learning rates. For context, the regular stochastic gradient descent (SGD) update of a model’s parameters θ𝜃\theta at time step t𝑡t looks like the following Ruder (2016):
items.zh: 与对模型所有层使用相同学习率不同，判别式微调允许我们以不同的学习率调整每一层。作为背景，模型参数θ在时间步t的常规随机梯度下降（SGD）更新如下所示（Ruder, 2016）：

items.en: where η𝜂\eta is the learning rate and ∇θJ​(θ)subscript∇𝜃𝐽𝜃\nabla_{\theta}J(\theta) is the gradient with regard to the model’s objective function. For discriminative fine-tuning, we split the parameters θ𝜃\theta into {θ1,…,θL}superscript𝜃1…superscript𝜃𝐿\{\theta^{1},\ldots,\theta^{L}\} where θlsuperscript𝜃𝑙\theta^{l} contains the parameters of the model at the l𝑙l-th layer and L𝐿L is the number of layers of the model. Similarly, we obtain {η1,…,ηL}superscript𝜂1…superscript𝜂𝐿\{\eta^{1},\ldots,\eta^{L}\} where ηlsuperscript𝜂𝑙\eta^{l} is the learning rate of the l𝑙l-th layer.
items.zh: 其中η是学习率，∇θJ(θ)是相对于模型目标函数的梯度。对于判别式微调，我们将参数θ拆分为{θ¹, …, θ^L}，其中θ^l包含模型第l层的参数，L是模型的层数。类似地，我们得到{η¹, …, η^L}，其中η^l是第l层的学习率。

items.en: The SGD update with discriminative fine-tuning is then the following:
items.zh: 使用判别式微调的SGD更新如下：

items.en: We empirically found it to work well to first choose the learning rate ηLsuperscript𝜂𝐿\eta^{L} of the last layer by fine-tuning only the last layer and using ηl−1=ηl/2.6superscript𝜂𝑙1superscript𝜂𝑙2.6\eta^{l-1}=\eta^{l}/2.6 as the learning rate for lower layers.
items.zh: 我们凭经验发现，首先通过仅微调最后一层来选择最后一层的学习率η^L，并使用η^{l-1} = η^l / 2.6作为较低层的学习率，效果很好。

items.en: For adapting its parameters to task-specific features, we would like the model to quickly converge to a suitable region of the parameter space in the beginning of training and then refine its parameters. Using the same learning rate (LR) or an annealed learning rate throughout training is not the best way to achieve this behaviour. Instead, we propose slanted triangular learning rates (STLR), which first linearly increases the learning rate and then linearly decays it according to the following update schedule, which can be seen in Figure 2:
items.zh: 为了使其参数适应任务特定的特征，我们希望模型在训练初期快速收敛到参数空间的合适区域，然后细化其参数。在整个训练过程中使用相同的学习率或衰减的学习率并不是实现这一行为的最佳方式。相反，我们提出了倾斜三角学习率（STLR），它首先线性增加学习率，然后根据以下更新计划线性衰减，如图2所示：

items.en: where T𝑇T is the number of training iterations555In other words, the number of epochs times the number of updates per epoch., c​u​t​_​f​r​a​c𝑐𝑢𝑡_𝑓𝑟𝑎𝑐cut\_frac is the fraction of iterations we increase the LR, c​u​t𝑐𝑢𝑡cut is the iteration when we switch from increasing to decreasing the LR, p𝑝p is the fraction of the number of iterations we have increased or will decrease the LR respectively, r​a​t​i​o𝑟𝑎𝑡𝑖𝑜ratio specifies how much smaller the lowest LR is from the maximum LR ηm​a​xsubscript𝜂𝑚𝑎𝑥\eta_{max}, and ηtsubscript𝜂𝑡\eta_{t} is the learning rate at iteration t𝑡t. We generally use c​u​t​_​f​r​a​c=0.1𝑐𝑢𝑡_𝑓𝑟𝑎𝑐0.1cut\_frac=0.1, r​a​t​i​o=32𝑟𝑎𝑡𝑖𝑜32ratio=32 and ηm​a​x=0.01subscript𝜂𝑚𝑎𝑥0.01\eta_{max}=0.01.
items.zh: 其中T是训练迭代次数，cut_frac是我们增加学习率的迭代比例，cut是我们从增加切换到减少学习率的迭代次数，p是我们已经增加或将要减少学习率的迭代次数的比例，ratio指定最低学习率与最大学习率η_max相比有多小，η_t是迭代t时的学习率。我们通常使用cut_frac=0.1，ratio=32和η_max=0.01。

items.en: STLR modifies triangular learning rates Smith (2017) with a short increase and a long decay period, which we found key for good performance.666We also credit personal communication with the author. In Section 5, we compare against aggressive cosine annealing, a similar schedule that has recently been used to achieve state-of-the-art performance in CV Loshchilov and Hutter (2017).777While Loshchilov and Hutter (2017) use multiple annealing cycles, we generally found one cycle to work best.
items.zh: STLR修改了三角学习率（Smith, 2017），具有短增加和长衰减周期，我们发现这对良好性能至关重要。在第5节中，我们与激进余弦退火进行了比较，这是一种类似的调度，最近被用于在计算机视觉中实现最先进的性能（Loshchilov and Hutter, 2017）。

items.capEn: Figure 2: The slanted triangular learning rate schedule used for ULMFiT as a function of the number of training iterations.
items.capZh: 图2：用于ULMFiT的倾斜三角学习率调度，作为训练迭代次数的函数。

items.en: Finally, for fine-tuning the classifier, we augment the pretrained language model with two additional linear blocks. Following standard practice for CV classifiers, each block uses batch normalization Ioffe and Szegedy (2015) and dropout, with ReLU activations for the intermediate layer and a softmax activation that outputs a probability distribution over target classes at the last layer. Note that the parameters in these task-specific classifier layers are the only ones that are learned from scratch. The first linear layer takes as the input the pooled last hidden layer states.
items.zh: 最后，为了微调分类器，我们用两个额外的线性块增强预训练的语言模型。遵循计算机视觉分类器的标准实践，每个块使用批归一化（Ioffe and Szegedy, 2015）和dropout，中间层使用ReLU激活，最后一层使用softmax激活，输出目标类上的概率分布。请注意，这些任务特定分类器层中的参数是唯一从头学习的参数。第一个线性层将池化的最后隐藏层状态作为输入。

items.en: The signal in text classification tasks is often contained in a few words, which may occur anywhere in the document. As input documents can consist of hundreds of words, information may get lost if we only consider the last hidden state of the model. For this reason, we concatenate the hidden state at the last time step 𝐡Tsubscript𝐡𝑇\mathbf{h}_{T} of the document with both the max-pooled and the mean-pooled representation of the hidden states over as many time steps as fit in GPU memory 𝐇={𝐡1,…,𝐡T}𝐇subscript𝐡1…subscript𝐡𝑇\mathbf{H}=\{\mathbf{h}_{1},\ldots,\mathbf{h}_{T}\}:
items.zh: 文本分类任务中的信号通常包含在几个词中，这些词可能出现在文档的任何位置。由于输入文档可能包含数百个词，如果我们只考虑模型的最后隐藏状态，信息可能会丢失。因此，我们将文档最后时间步的隐藏状态h_T与在GPU内存允许的尽可能多时间步上的隐藏状态的最大池化和平均池化表示H = {h_1, …, h_T}连接起来：

items.en: Fine-tuning the target classifier is the most critical part of the transfer learning method. Overly aggressive fine-tuning will cause catastrophic forgetting, eliminating the benefit of the information captured through language modeling; too cautious fine-tuning will lead to slow convergence (and resultant overfitting). Besides discriminative fine-tuning and triangular learning rates, we propose gradual unfreezing for fine-tuning the classifier.
items.zh: 微调目标分类器是迁移学习方法中最关键的部分。过于激进的微调会导致灾难性遗忘，消除通过语言建模捕获的信息的好处；过于谨慎的微调会导致收敛缓慢（以及随之而来的过拟合）。除了判别式微调和三角学习率，我们还提出了逐步解冻来微调分类器。

items.en: Rather than fine-tuning all layers at once, which risks catastrophic forgetting, we propose to gradually unfreeze the model starting from the last layer as this contains the least general knowledge Yosinski et al. (2014): We first unfreeze the last layer and fine-tune all unfrozen layers for one epoch. We then unfreeze the next lower frozen layer and repeat, until we fine-tune all layers until convergence at the last iteration. This is similar to ‘chain-thaw’ Felbo et al. (2017), except that we add a layer at a time to the set of ‘thawed’ layers, rather than only training a single layer at a time.
items.zh: 与其一次性微调所有层（这有灾难性遗忘的风险），我们建议从最后一层开始逐步解冻模型，因为这一层包含最少的通用知识（Yosinski et al., 2014）：我们首先解冻最后一层，并微调所有解冻的层一个epoch。然后我们解冻下一个较低的冻结层并重复，直到我们在最后一次迭代中微调所有层直至收敛。这类似于'chain-thaw'（Felbo et al., 2017），不同之处在于我们一次向'解冻'层集合中添加一层，而不是一次只训练一层。

items.en: While discriminative fine-tuning, slanted triangular learning rates, and gradual unfreezing all are beneficial on their own, we show in Section 5 that they complement each other and enable our method to perform well across diverse datasets.
items.zh: 尽管判别式微调、倾斜三角学习率和逐步解冻各自都有益，但在第5节中，我们展示了它们相互补充，使我们的方法能够在不同数据集上表现良好。

items.en: Language models are trained with backpropagation through time (BPTT) to enable gradient propagation for large input sequences. In order to make fine-tuning a classifier for large documents feasible, we propose BPTT for Text Classification (BPT3C): We divide the document into fixed-length batches of size b𝑏b. At the beginning of each batch, the model is initialized with the final state of the previous batch; we keep track of the hidden states for mean and max-pooling; gradients are back-propagated to the batches whose hidden states contributed to the final prediction. In practice, we use variable length backpropagation sequences Merity et al. (2017a).
items.zh: 语言模型通过时间反向传播（BPTT）进行训练，以实现大输入序列的梯度传播。为了使大型文档的分类器微调可行，我们提出了用于文本分类的BPTT（BPT3C）：我们将文档划分为固定长度的批次，大小为b。在每个批次开始时，模型用前一批次的最终状态初始化；我们跟踪用于均值和最大池化的隐藏状态；梯度反向传播到其隐藏状态对最终预测有贡献的批次。在实践中，我们使用可变长度的反向传播序列（Merity et al., 2017a）。

items.en: Similar to existing work Peters et al. (2017, 2018), we are not limited to fine-tuning a unidirectional language model. For all our experiments, we pretrain both a forward and a backward LM. We fine-tune a classifier for each LM independently using BPT3C and average the classifier predictions.
items.zh: 与现有工作（Peters et al., 2017, 2018）类似，我们不仅限于微调单向语言模型。在我们所有的实验中，我们预训练了前向和后向语言模型。我们使用BPT3C独立地为每个语言模型微调一个分类器，并对分类器预测进行平均。

items.en: While our approach is equally applicable to sequence labeling tasks, we focus on text classification tasks in this work due to their important real-world applications.
items.zh: 虽然我们的方法同样适用于序列标注任务，但在这项工作中，我们专注于文本分类任务，因为它们具有重要的实际应用。

items.en: We evaluate our method on six widely-studied datasets, with varying numbers of documents and varying document length, used by state-of-the-art text classification and transfer learning approaches Johnson and Zhang (2017); McCann et al. (2017) as instances of three common text classification tasks: sentiment analysis, question classification, and topic classification. We show the statistics for each dataset and task in Table 1.
items.zh: 我们在六个广泛研究的数据集上评估我们的方法，这些数据集具有不同数量的文档和不同的文档长度，被最先进的文本分类和迁移学习方法（Johnson and Zhang, 2017; McCann et al., 2017）用作三种常见文本分类任务的实例：情感分析、问题分类和主题分类。我们在表1中显示了每个数据集和任务的统计信息。

items.en: For sentiment analysis, we evaluate our approach on the binary movie review IMDb dataset Maas et al. (2011) and on the binary and five-class version of the Yelp review dataset compiled by Zhang et al. (2015).
items.zh: 对于情感分析，我们在二分类电影评论IMDb数据集（Maas et al., 2011）以及由Zhang等人（2015）编译的Yelp评论数据集的二分类和五分类版本上评估我们的方法。

items.en: We use the six-class version of the small TREC dataset Voorhees and Tice (1999) dataset of open-domain, fact-based questions divided into broad semantic categories.
items.zh: 我们使用小型TREC数据集（Voorhees和Tice，1999）的六类版本，该数据集包含开放域、基于事实的问题，并划分为广泛的语义类别。

items.en: For topic classification, we evaluate on the large-scale AG news and DBpedia ontology datasets created by Zhang et al. (2015).
items.zh: 对于主题分类，我们在由Zhang等人（2015）创建的大规模AG新闻和DBpedia本体数据集上进行评估。

items.en: We use the same pre-processing as in earlier work Johnson and Zhang (2017); McCann et al. (2017). In addition, to allow the language model to capture aspects that might be relevant for classification, we add special tokens for upper-case words, elongation, and repetition.
items.zh: 我们采用与早期工作（Johnson和Zhang，2017；McCann等人，2017）相同的预处理。此外，为了让语言模型捕捉可能对分类相关的方面，我们为大写单词、延长和重复添加了特殊标记。

items.en: We are interested in a model that performs robustly across a diverse set of tasks. To this end, if not mentioned otherwise, we use the same set of hyperparameters across tasks, which we tune on the IMDb validation set. We use the AWD-LSTM language model Merity et al. (2017a) with an embedding size of 400400400, 333 layers, 115011501150 hidden activations per layer, and a BPTT batch size of 707070. We apply dropout of 0.40.40.4 to layers, 0.30.30.3 to RNN layers, 0.40.40.4 to input embedding layers, 0.050.050.05 to embedding layers, and weight dropout of 0.50.50.5 to the RNN hidden-to-hidden matrix. The classifier has a hidden layer of size 505050. We use Adam with β1=0.7subscript𝛽10.7\beta_{1}=0.7 instead of the default β1=0.9subscript𝛽10.9\beta_{1}=0.9 and β2=0.99subscript𝛽20.99\beta_{2}=0.99, similar to Dozat and Manning (2017). We use a batch size of 646464, a base learning rate of 0.0040.0040.004 and 0.010.010.01 for fine-tuning the LM and the classifier respectively, and tune the number of epochs on the validation set of each task888On small datasets such as TREC-6, we fine-tune the LM only for 151515 epochs without overfitting, while we can fine-tune longer on larger datasets. We found 505050 epochs to be a good default for fine-tuning the classifier.. We otherwise use the same practices used in Merity et al. (2017a).
items.zh: 我们关注的是在多样化任务中表现稳健的模型。为此，除非另有说明，我们在所有任务中使用相同的超参数集，并在IMDb验证集上进行调优。我们使用AWD-LSTM语言模型（Merity等人，2017a），嵌入大小为400，3层，每层1150个隐藏激活，BPTT批大小为70。我们对层应用0.4的dropout，对RNN层应用0.3，对输入嵌入层应用0.4，对嵌入层应用0.05，并对RNN隐藏到隐藏矩阵应用0.5的权重dropout。分类器有一个大小为50的隐藏层。我们使用Adam优化器，β₁=0.7而不是默认的β₁=0.9，β₂=0.99，类似于Dozat和Manning（2017）。我们使用批大小为64，基础学习率分别为0.004（用于微调LM）和0.01（用于微调分类器），并在每个任务的验证集上调整训练轮数。对于小型数据集（如TREC-6），我们仅微调LM 15轮而不过拟合，而在较大数据集上可以微调更长时间。我们发现50轮是微调分类器的良好默认值。除此之外，我们使用与Merity等人（2017a）相同的实践。

items.en: For each task, we compare against the current state-of-the-art. For the IMDb and TREC-6 datasets, we compare against CoVe McCann et al. (2017), a state-of-the-art transfer learning method for NLP. For the AG, Yelp, and DBpedia datasets, we compare against the state-of-the-art text categorization method by Johnson and Zhang (2017).
items.zh: 对于每个任务，我们与当前最先进的方法进行比较。对于IMDb和TREC-6数据集，我们与CoVe（McCann等人，2017）进行比较，这是一种最先进的NLP迁移学习方法。对于AG、Yelp和DBpedia数据集，我们与Johnson和Zhang（2017）的最先进文本分类方法进行比较。

items.en: For consistency, we report all results as error rates (lower is better). We show the test error rates on the IMDb and TREC-6 datasets used by McCann et al. (2017) in Table 2. Our method outperforms both CoVe, a state-of-the-art transfer learning method based on hypercolumns, as well as the state-of-the-art on both datasets. On IMDb, we reduce the error dramatically by 43.9% and 22% with regard to CoVe and the state-of-the-art respectively. This is promising as the existing state-of-the-art requires complex architectures Peters et al. (2018), multiple forms of attention McCann et al. (2017) and sophisticated embedding schemes Johnson and Zhang (2016), while our method employs a regular LSTM with dropout. We note that the language model fine-tuning approach of Dai and Le (2015) only achieves an error of 7.64 vs. 4.6 for our method on IMDb, demonstrating the benefit of transferring knowledge from a large ImageNet-like corpus using our fine-tuning techniques. IMDb in particular is reflective of real-world datasets: Its documents are generally a few paragraphs long—similar to emails (e.g for legal discovery) and online comments (e.g for community management); and sentiment analysis is similar to many commercial applications, e.g. product response tracking and support email routing.
items.zh: 为了一致性，我们将所有结果报告为错误率（越低越好）。我们在表2中展示了McCann等人（2017）使用的IMDb和TREC-6数据集的测试错误率。我们的方法在两个数据集上均优于CoVe（一种基于超列的最先进迁移学习方法）和最先进方法。在IMDb上，与CoVe和最先进方法相比，我们分别将错误率大幅降低了43.9%和22%。这很有前景，因为现有的最先进方法需要复杂的架构（Peters等人，2018）、多种形式的注意力机制（McCann等人，2017）和复杂的嵌入方案（Johnson和Zhang，2016），而我们的方法仅使用带有dropout的常规LSTM。我们注意到，Dai和Le（2015）的语言模型微调方法在IMDb上仅达到7.64的错误率，而我们的方法为4.6，这证明了使用我们的微调技术从类似ImageNet的大规模语料库迁移知识的好处。IMDb特别能反映真实世界的数据集：其文档通常为几段长——类似于电子邮件（例如用于法律发现）和在线评论（例如用于社区管理）；情感分析类似于许多商业应用，例如产品响应跟踪和支持邮件路由。

items.en: On TREC-6, our improvement—similar as the improvements of state-of-the-art approaches—is not statistically significant, due to the small size of the 500-examples test set. Nevertheless, the competitive performance on TREC-6 demonstrates that our model performs well across different dataset sizes and can deal with examples that range from single sentences—in the case of TREC-6—to several paragraphs for IMDb. Note that despite pretraining on more than two orders of magnitude less data than the 7 million sentence pairs used by McCann et al. (2017), we consistently outperform their approach on both datasets.
items.zh: 在TREC-6上，我们的改进——类似于最先进方法的改进——在统计上不显著，因为测试集只有500个样本，规模较小。尽管如此，在TREC-6上的竞争性能表明，我们的模型在不同数据集大小上表现良好，并且能够处理从单句（TREC-6）到多段（IMDb）的样本。请注意，尽管预训练数据比McCann等人（2017）使用的700万句子对少两个数量级以上，我们在两个数据集上始终优于他们的方法。

items.en: We show the test error rates on the larger AG, DBpedia, Yelp-bi, and Yelp-full datasets in Table 3. Our method again outperforms the state-of-the-art significantly. On AG, we observe a similarly dramatic error reduction by 23.7% compared to the state-of-the-art. On DBpedia, Yelp-bi, and Yelp-full, we reduce the error by 4.8%, 18.2%, 2.0% respectively.
items.zh: 我们在表3中展示了较大数据集AG、DBpedia、Yelp-bi和Yelp-full上的测试错误率。我们的方法再次显著优于最先进方法。在AG上，我们观察到与最先进方法相比，错误率大幅降低了23.7%。在DBpedia、Yelp-bi和Yelp-full上，我们分别将错误率降低了4.8%、18.2%和2.0%。

items.en: In order to assess the impact of each contribution, we perform a series of analyses and ablations. We run experiments on three corpora, IMDb, TREC-6, and AG that are representative of different tasks, genres, and sizes. For all experiments, we split off 10%percent1010\% of the training set and report error rates on this validation set with unidirectional LMs. We fine-tune the classifier for 505050 epochs and train all methods but ULMFiT with early stopping.
items.zh: 为了评估每项贡献的影响，我们进行了一系列分析和消融实验。我们在三个语料库（IMDb、TREC-6和AG）上进行实验，这些语料库代表了不同的任务、体裁和规模。对于所有实验，我们分割出10%的训练集，并在该验证集上使用单向LM报告错误率。我们微调分类器50轮，并使用早停法训练除ULMFiT之外的所有方法。

items.en: One of the main benefits of transfer learning is being able to train a model for a task with a small number of labels. We evaluate ULMFiT on different numbers of labeled examples in two settings: only labeled examples are used for LM fine-tuning (‘supervised’); and all task data is available and can be used to fine-tune the LM (‘semi-supervised’). We compare ULMFiT to training from scratch—which is necessary for hypercolumn-based approaches. We split off balanced fractions of the training data, keep the validation set fixed, and use the same hyperparameters as before. We show the results in Figure 3.
items.zh: 迁移学习的主要好处之一是能够使用少量标签训练模型。我们在两种设置下评估ULMFiT在不同标记样本数量上的表现：仅使用标记样本进行LM微调（“监督”）；以及所有任务数据可用并可用于微调LM（“半监督”）。我们将ULMFiT与从头训练进行比较——这对于基于超列的方法是必要的。我们分割出平衡的训练数据比例，保持验证集固定，并使用与之前相同的超参数。结果如图3所示。

items.en: On IMDb and AG, supervised ULMFiT with only 100100100 labeled examples matches the performance of training from scratch with 10×10\times and 20×20\times more data respectively, clearly demonstrating the benefit of general-domain LM pretraining. If we allow ULMFiT to also utilize unlabeled examples (505050k for IMDb, 100100100k for AG), at 100100100 labeled examples, we match the performance of training from scratch with 50×50\times and 100×100\times more data on AG and IMDb respectively. On TREC-6, ULMFiT significantly improves upon training from scratch; as examples are shorter and fewer, supervised and semi-supervised ULMFiT achieve similar results.
items.zh: 在IMDb和AG上，仅使用100个标记样本的监督ULMFiT分别匹配了使用10倍和20倍更多数据的从头训练的性能，清楚地证明了通用领域LM预训练的好处。如果我们允许ULMFiT也利用未标记样本（IMDb为50k，AG为100k），在100个标记样本下，我们在AG和IMDb上分别匹配了使用50倍和100倍更多数据的从头训练的性能。在TREC-6上，ULMFiT显著优于从头训练；由于样本较短且较少，监督和半监督ULMFiT取得了相似的结果。

items.en: We compare using no pretraining with pretraining on WikiText-103 Merity et al. (2017b) in Table 4. Pretraining is most useful for small and medium-sized datasets, which are most common in commercial applications. However, even for large datasets, pretraining improves performance.
items.zh: 我们在表4中比较了无预训练与在WikiText-103（Merity等人，2017b）上预训练的效果。预训练对于小型和中型数据集最为有用，这些数据集在商业应用中最常见。然而，即使对于大型数据集，预训练也能提高性能。

items.en: In order to gauge the importance of choosing an appropriate LM, we compare a vanilla LM with the same hyperparameters without any dropout999To avoid overfitting, we only train the vanilla LM classifier for 555 epochs and keep dropout of 0.40.40.4 in the classifier. with the AWD-LSTM LM with tuned dropout parameters in Table 5. Using our fine-tuning techniques, even a regular LM reaches surprisingly good performance on the larger datasets. On the smaller TREC-6, a vanilla LM without dropout runs the risk of overfitting, which decreases performance.
items.zh: 为了评估选择合适LM的重要性，我们在表5中将普通LM（相同超参数，无dropout）与具有调优dropout参数的AWD-LSTM LM进行比较。使用我们的微调技术，即使是普通LM在较大数据集上也达到了令人惊讶的良好性能。在较小的TREC-6上，无dropout的普通LM存在过拟合风险，从而降低性能。

items.en: We compare no fine-tuning against fine-tuning the full model Erhan et al. (2010) (‘Full’), the most commonly used fine-tuning method, with and without discriminative fine-tuning (‘Discr’) and slanted triangular learning rates (‘Stlr’) in Table 6. Fine-tuning the LM is most beneficial for larger datasets. ‘Discr’ and ‘Stlr’ improve performance across all three datasets and are necessary on the smaller TREC-6, where regular fine-tuning is not beneficial.
items.zh: 我们在表6中比较了无微调与全模型微调（Erhan等人，2010）（“Full”）（最常用的微调方法）、带判别微调（“Discr”）和带倾斜三角学习率（“Stlr”）的情况。LM微调对较大数据集最有益。“Discr”和“Stlr”在所有三个数据集上提高了性能，并且在较小的TREC-6上是必要的，因为常规微调没有益处。

items.en: We compare training from scratch, fine-tuning the full model (‘Full’), only fine-tuning the last layer (‘Last’) Donahue et al. (2014), ‘Chain-thaw’ Felbo et al. (2017), and gradual unfreezing (‘Freez’). We furthermore assess the importance of discriminative fine-tuning (‘Discr’) and slanted triangular learning rates (‘Stlr’). We compare the latter to an alternative, aggressive cosine annealing schedule (‘Cos’) Loshchilov and Hutter (2017). We use a learning rate ηL=0.01superscript𝜂𝐿0.01\eta^{L}=0.01 for ‘Discr’, learning rates of 0.0010.0010.001 and 0.00010.00010.0001 for the last and all other layers respectively for ‘Chain-thaw’ as in Felbo et al. (2017), and a learning rate of 0.0010.0010.001 otherwise. We show the results in Table 7.
items.zh: 我们比较了从头训练、全模型微调（“Full”）、仅微调最后一层（“Last”）（Donahue等人，2014）、“Chain-thaw”（Felbo等人，2017）和逐步解冻（“Freez”）。此外，我们评估了判别微调（“Discr”）和倾斜三角学习率（“Stlr”）的重要性。我们将后者与另一种激进的余弦退火调度（“Cos”）（Loshchilov和Hutter，2017）进行比较。对于“Discr”，我们使用学习率η^L=0.01；对于“Chain-thaw”，最后一层学习率为0.001，其他层为0.0001，如Felbo等人（2017）所述；其他情况学习率为0.001。结果如表7所示。

items.en: Fine-tuning the classifier significantly improves over training from scratch, particularly on the small TREC-6. ‘Last’, the standard fine-tuning method in CV, severely underfits and is never able to lower the training error to 00. ‘Chain-thaw’ achieves competitive performance on the smaller datasets, but is outperformed significantly on the large AG. ‘Freez’ provides similar performance as ‘Full’. ‘Discr’ consistently boosts the performance of ‘Full’ and ‘Freez’, except for the large AG. Cosine annealing is competitive with slanted triangular learning rates on large data, but under-performs on smaller datasets. Finally, full ULMFiT classifier fine-tuning (bottom row) achieves the best performance on IMDB and TREC-6 and competitive performance on AG. Importantly, ULMFiT is the only method that shows excellent performance across the board—and is therefore the only universal method.
items.zh: 微调分类器显著优于从头训练，尤其是在小型TREC-6上。“Last”是CV中的标准微调方法，但严重欠拟合，从未能将训练误差降至0。“Chain-thaw”在较小数据集上取得了竞争性能，但在大型AG上显著逊色。“Freez”提供了与“Full”相似的性能。“Discr”持续提升了“Full”和“Freez”的性能，除了大型AG。余弦退火在大型数据上与倾斜三角学习率具有竞争力，但在较小数据集上表现不佳。最后，完整的ULMFiT分类器微调（最后一行）在IMDB和TREC-6上取得了最佳性能，在AG上具有竞争性能。重要的是，ULMFiT是唯一在所有数据集上表现出色的方法——因此是唯一通用的方法。

items.en: While our results demonstrate that how we fine-tune the classifier makes a significant difference, fine-tuning for inductive transfer is currently under-explored in NLP as it mostly has been thought to be unhelpful Mou et al. (2016). To better understand the fine-tuning behavior of our model, we compare the validation error of the classifier fine-tuned with ULMFiT and ‘Full’ during training in Figure 4.
items.zh: 虽然我们的结果表明分类器微调的方式有显著影响，但用于归纳迁移的微调在NLP中目前探索不足，因为人们通常认为它无益（Mou等人，2016）。为了更好地理解我们模型的微调行为，我们在图4中比较了使用ULMFiT和“Full”微调的分类器在训练过程中的验证误差。

items.en: On all datasets, fine-tuning the full model leads to the lowest error comparatively early in training, e.g. already after the first epoch on IMDb. The error then increases as the model starts to overfit and knowledge captured through pretraining is lost. In contrast, ULMFiT is more stable and suffers from no such catastrophic forgetting; performance remains similar or improves until late epochs, which shows the positive effect of the learning rate schedule.
items.zh: 在所有数据集上，微调完整模型在训练早期就能达到最低误差，例如在IMDb上第一个epoch后即如此。随后，随着模型开始过拟合且预训练捕获的知识丢失，误差增加。相比之下，ULMFiT更稳定，不会出现这种灾难性遗忘；性能在后期epoch中保持相似或提升，这显示了学习率调度的积极作用。

items.en: At the cost of training a second model, ensembling the predictions of a forward and backwards LM-classifier brings a performance boost of around 0.50.50.5–0.70.70.7. On IMDb we lower the test error from 5.305.305.30 of a single model to 4.584.584.58 for the bidirectional model.
items.zh: 以训练第二个模型为代价，集成前向和后向LM分类器的预测可带来约0.5–0.7的性能提升。在IMDb上，我们将测试误差从单个模型的5.30降低到双向模型的4.58。

items.en: While we have shown that ULMFiT can achieve state-of-the-art performance on widely used text classification tasks, we believe that language model fine-tuning will be particularly useful in the following settings compared to existing transfer learning approaches Conneau et al. (2017); McCann et al. (2017); Peters et al. (2018): a) NLP for non-English languages, where training data for supervised pretraining tasks is scarce; b) new NLP tasks where no state-of-the-art architecture exists; and c) tasks with limited amounts of labeled data (and some amounts of unlabeled data).
items.zh: 虽然我们已经证明ULMFiT在广泛使用的文本分类任务上能够达到最先进的性能，但我们相信，与现有的迁移学习方法Conneau等人(2017); McCann等人(2017); Peters等人(2018)相比，语言模型微调在以下场景中将特别有用：a) 非英语语言的NLP，其中用于监督预训练任务的训练数据稀缺；b) 新的NLP任务，尚无最先进的架构；c) 标注数据有限（以及一些未标注数据）的任务。

items.en: Given that transfer learning and particularly fine-tuning for NLP is under-explored, many future directions are possible. One possible direction is to improve language model pretraining and fine-tuning and make them more scalable: for ImageNet, predicting far fewer classes only incurs a small performance drop Huh et al. (2016), while recent work shows that an alignment between source and target task label sets is important Mahajan et al. (2018)—focusing on predicting a subset of words such as the most frequent ones might retain most of the performance while speeding up training. Language modeling can also be augmented with additional tasks in a multi-task learning fashion Caruana (1993) or enriched with additional supervision, e.g. syntax-sensitive dependencies Linzen et al. (2016) to create a model that is more general or better suited for certain downstream tasks, ideally in a weakly-supervised manner to retain its universal properties.
items.zh: 鉴于NLP中的迁移学习，特别是微调，尚未得到充分探索，许多未来方向是可能的。一个可能的方向是改进语言模型预训练和微调，使其更具可扩展性：对于ImageNet，预测更少的类别只会导致较小的性能下降Huh等人(2016)，而最近的工作表明源任务和目标任务标签集之间的对齐很重要Mahajan等人(2018)——专注于预测最频繁的词子集可能保留大部分性能，同时加速训练。语言建模还可以通过多任务学习的方式Caruana(1993)增加额外任务，或通过额外的监督（例如句法敏感依赖Linzen等人(2016)）来丰富，以创建更通用或更适合某些下游任务的模型，理想情况下以弱监督方式保留其通用属性。

items.en: Another direction is to apply the method to novel tasks and models. While an extension to sequence labeling is straightforward, other tasks with more complex interactions such as entailment or question answering may require novel ways to pretrain and fine-tune. Finally, while we have provided a series of analyses and ablations, more studies are required to better understand what knowledge a pretrained language model captures, how this changes during fine-tuning, and what information different tasks require.
items.zh: 另一个方向是将该方法应用于新任务和模型。虽然扩展到序列标注是直接的，但其他具有更复杂交互的任务，如蕴含或问答，可能需要新的预训练和微调方式。最后，虽然我们提供了一系列分析和消融研究，但仍需更多研究来更好地理解预训练语言模型捕获了哪些知识，这些知识在微调过程中如何变化，以及不同任务需要哪些信息。

items.en: We have proposed ULMFiT, an effective and extremely sample-efficient transfer learning method that can be applied to any NLP task. We have also proposed several novel fine-tuning techniques that in conjunction prevent catastrophic forgetting and enable robust learning across a diverse range of tasks. Our method significantly outperformed existing transfer learning techniques and the state-of-the-art on six representative text classification tasks. We hope that our results will catalyze new developments in transfer learning for NLP.
items.zh: 我们提出了ULMFiT，一种有效且极其样本高效的迁移学习方法，可应用于任何NLP任务。我们还提出了几种新颖的微调技术，它们共同防止灾难性遗忘，并实现在各种任务上的鲁棒学习。我们的方法显著优于现有的迁移学习技术和六个代表性文本分类任务上的最先进水平。我们希望我们的结果将推动NLP迁移学习的新发展。

items.en: We thank the anonymous reviewers for their valuable feedback. Sebastian is supported by Irish Research Council Grant Number EBPPG/2014/30 and Science Foundation Ireland Grant Number SFI/12/RC/2289.
items.zh: 我们感谢匿名审稿人的宝贵反馈。Sebastian得到爱尔兰研究委员会资助号EBPPG/2014/30和爱尔兰科学基金会资助号SFI/12/RC/2289的支持。
